answer:
We need \( \sin(7\pi \sin(5x)) = 0 \), so  
\[
7\pi \sin(5x) = k\pi \quad \Rightarrow \quad \sin(5x) = \frac{k}{7}
\]  
for integer \(k\). Since \(|\sin(5x)| \le 1\), \(k = -7,-6,\dots,7\).

For each \(k\), \(\sin(5x) = k/7\) gives two solutions for \(5x\) in \([0,2\pi)\) except when \(|k|=7\) (one solution each) or \(k=0\) (four solutions for \(5x\) in \([0,2\pi)\)).  
Counting: For \(k = \pm1,\pm2,\pm3,\pm4,\pm5,\pm6\): \(12\) values of \(k\) each give \(2\) solutions for \(5x\) → \(24\)
